skill.name: testing-strategy-builder
skill.description: Build practical testing strategies for software projects using templates, coverage targets, test case structures, and guidance for unit, integration, end-to-end, and performance testing.
skill.summary: Create test plans, coverage goals, and QA workflows for application development.
skill.author: AI Agent Hub

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto patterns, but reviewed evidence shows they are Markdown examples, templates, and testing guidance rather than executable skill logic. No prompt-injection language, data exfiltration intent, or malicious automation was found in the reviewed files. The skill is suitable for publication with low risk because users may copy commands or sample tests into their own projects.

low_findings.title: External Command Patterns Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The flagged command patterns appear in Markdown installation commands, verification commands, CI examples, and code fences. They are not executed by the skill and do not include user-controlled command construction.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are visible Markdown guidance or fenced examples. No executable script file or dynamic command invocation was found in these reviewed locations.

low_findings.title: Network Patterns Are Sample Test Requests
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The HTTP client and URL findings are example API tests and a k6 load-test sample. The skill does not send data externally or configure a real destination for exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed network references are inside test examples and target local app routes or example.com. There is no evidence of credential collection or outbound calls by the skill itself.

low_findings.title: Filesystem And Weak-Crypto Alerts Lack Risk Context
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The path traversal finding is a Jest mock import in a code example, and weak-crypto alerts occur at general testing text or sample data locations. No filesystem access routine or cryptographic implementation was found at the cited locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited path is a relative mock path inside an illustrative test. Reviewed weak-crypto locations do not contain crypto API use, key handling, or hashing implementation.

content.value_statement: Teams often miss coverage gaps, unclear test ownership, and weak release gates. This skill provides structured plans, templates, and checklists for reliable software testing.
actual_capabilities: Defines coverage targets for unit, integration, end-to-end, performance, and security testing.
Provides a reusable test plan template for scope, environments, schedules, metrics, and approvals.
Provides a test case template with preconditions, steps, validation checkpoints, and execution history.
Offers example test patterns for Jest, Playwright, Supertest, k6, fixtures, factories, and mocking.
Includes a coverage checklist for static analysis, APIs, databases, user journeys, and release gates.
Helps prioritize tests with risk-based coverage guidance for critical and lower-risk features.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not an automated test runner.
Users must adapt examples to their framework, architecture, and compliance requirements.
Coverage targets are recommendations and may not fit every product risk profile.
Sample commands and code should be reviewed before use in production pipelines.

use_cases.title: Plan Tests For A New Feature
use_cases.description: Create a test plan with scope, risk levels, coverage targets, test types, and release criteria before implementation begins.

use_cases.title: Improve Coverage In An Existing App
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to find missing unit, integration, end-to-end, performance, accessibility, and security test areas.

use_cases.title: Standardize Test Case Documentation
use_cases.description: Create consistent test cases with preconditions, steps, expected results, validation checkpoints, defects, and sign-off records.

prompt_templates.title: Create A Basic Test Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a test plan for this feature: [feature]. Include scope, test types, coverage targets, environments, and exit criteria.

prompt_templates.title: Find Coverage Gaps
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this project summary and identify likely testing gaps. Separate gaps by unit, integration, end-to-end, performance, security, and accessibility tests.

prompt_templates.title: Write Risk-Based Test Cases
prompt_templates.prompt: Create risk-based test cases for [workflow]. Include critical, high, medium, and low priority scenarios with expected results.

prompt_templates.title: Design A Full Testing Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete testing strategy for [application]. Include tooling, test data, CI gates, coverage metrics, ownership, reporting, and rollout risks.

output_examples.input: A checkout feature needs release testing before launch.
output: A risk-based test plan covering login, cart updates, payment success, payment failure, order confirmation, and email delivery.
Coverage targets for critical payment paths, API integration points, and browser-based end-to-end flows.
Exit criteria that block release on failed payment, inventory, or order persistence tests.

output_examples.input: A team has low confidence in its current coverage.
output: A coverage gap review grouped by business logic, API contracts, database behavior, user journeys, security checks, and CI reporting.
A prioritized improvement plan that starts with critical paths and adds quality gates after stable baseline coverage exists.

output_examples.input: A QA team needs consistent manual and automated test case records.
output: A reusable test case format with objective, preconditions, test data, steps, expected results, validation checkpoints, and sign-off fields.
Clear sections for defects, execution history, dependencies, and release approval evidence.

best_practices: Start with risk-based priorities, then assign coverage targets to critical paths and lower-risk areas.
Keep tests focused on behavior, observable results, and clear assertions instead of implementation details.
Run fast checks on every commit and reserve slower end-to-end or performance suites for pull requests and releases.
anti_patterns: Do not treat high coverage as proof that important user workflows are safe.
Do not build large tests that mix setup, many workflows, and unrelated assertions.
Do not copy sample commands or test data into production systems without review.

faq.question: What does this skill help create?
faq.answer: It helps create testing strategies, test plans, test cases, coverage targets, and QA checklists for software projects.

faq.question: Does it run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance, templates, and examples. Users run their own tools in their project environment.

faq.question: Which test types does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers static analysis, unit tests, integration tests, end-to-end tests, performance tests, security checks, and accessibility checks.

faq.question: Can it support both frontend and backend teams?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for UI workflows, API contracts, database behavior, service integrations, and CI quality gates.

faq.question: Are the included examples production ready?
faq.answer: They are starting points. Users should adapt commands, code, credentials, and URLs before using them in real projects.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Provide the application type, critical workflows, technology stack, current test coverage, release risks, and compliance needs.
